name: Mocha Buttercrunch Pie
recipeIngredient: 2 ounces unsweetened baking chocolate, cut into bits
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
2. Prepare crust: In a small mixing bowl, cut shortening into flour until the mixture is the consistency of cornmeal. Add water and stir to combine; form a small ball of dough. In a food processor fitted with a metal blade, combine dough, brown sugar, pecans, chocolate chips, and vanilla. Process to chop the mixture coarsely. Expect some large chunks to be left.
3. Press the crumb mixture into a 9-inch pie pan to cover the bottom and half of the side. Bake for 6-8 minutes or until just brown. Allow the shell to cool completely.
4. Prepare filling: In a small saucepan over low heat, melt chocolate pieces in 1/4 cup cream, stirring frequently, until the mixture is smooth. Set aside to cool.
5. In a large bowl of mixer, combine the remaining whipping cream, powdered sugar, and coffee granules. Beat until cream thickens and forms peaks. Fold the chocolate mixture into whipped cream by hand. Do not over mix because the cream has a tendency to break up after chocolate is added. Pour filling into prepared pie crust, smoothing the top.
6. Freeze filling for at least 1 hour.
7. Prepare topping: In a large bowl of mixer, combine whipping cream, powdered sugar, and instant coffee granules. Beat at high speed until stiff. Mound over the filling, smoothing the surface. Sprinkle chocolate morsels over the top and freeze until solid. Serve frozen. Makes one 9-inch pie.
